DashboardSubtitle: Optimize your system and Rust for maximum performance.
HeroDescription: Rust Optimizer helps you get the best performance out of your system so you can focus on what matters:
GraphicsPageSubtitle: Simplified quality settings for Rust, grouped the way the in-game options menu does.
QualitySettingsSubtitle: Each slider adjusts several related in-game settings together. Pick Low, Medium, or High for the balance of quality vs. performance you want.
ProfilesSubtitle: Switch between quality profiles, or save your own. The three built-in presets can't be changed.
ProfileNamePrompt: Enter a name for this profile.
ProfileDeleteMessageFormat: This will permanently delete "{0}". This can't be undone.
UtilitiesPageSubtitle: Handy external tools and resources for Rust.
UtilitiesDisclaimer: Rust Optimizer is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or responsible for the content of any of these third-party sites.
UtilityRustHelpDescription: Community wiki and toolkit covering skins and monuments, with tools like a raid calculator and tech tree planner.
UtilityRustBreederDescription: Crossbreeding calculator for optimizing plant genetics like hemp and berries.
UtilityBattleMetricsDescription: Server browser, player tracking, and RCON-based server administration.
UtilityRustStatsDescription: Player statistics and leaderboards tracked via the Steam API, with Discord integration.
UtilityRustMapsDescription: Map viewer and generator for previewing procedural Rust maps and monuments.
UtilityCorrosionHourDescription: Rust news, guides, and community content.
UtilityOfficialWikiDescription: Facepunch's official wiki covering items, mechanics, and updates.
UtilityOfficialCommitsDescription: Live feed of Facepunch's development commits for Rust.
UtilityRustricianDescription: Electricity simulator for designing, testing, and sharing in-game circuits.
UtilityRaidboi1129Description: Mobile raid cost and loot calculator for Rust.
PowerPlanWarningTooltip: Not recommended - this plan can throttle performance. Switch to High performance or Ultimate Performance unless you need to save battery.
PointerPrecisionWarningTooltip: Not recommended for competitive play - mouse acceleration makes your aim less consistent. Leave it off unless another app needs it.
GameModeWarningTooltip: Recommended on - Game Mode helps Windows prioritize the game process. Turn it on unless you've had issues with it.
GameModeMedalDetectedTooltip: Medal is running and resets this back off itself a few seconds after every login. Check Medal's Settings > Windows OS Settings > Advanced Settings if you want it to stay on.
BackgroundRecordingWarningTooltip: Not recommended while gaming - background recording uses CPU/GPU resources. Turn it off unless you rely on Xbox Game Bar clips.
FullscreenOptimizationsWarningTooltip: Recommended on - fullscreen optimizations can add input latency. Turn this on unless you need quicker Alt+Tab switching.
MemorySizeWarningTooltip: Below the recommended amount - Rust performs best with at least 16 GB of RAM. Consider upgrading if you run into stutter or high memory usage.
MemorySpeedWarningTooltip: Running below its rated speed - enable XMP/EXPO in your BIOS to reach the RAM's full rated speed, unless you've deliberately underclocked it.
RefreshRateWarningTooltip: Running below its maximum refresh rate - check your monitor's display settings, unless you've deliberately capped it.
ResolutionWarningTooltip: Running below its maximum resolution - check your monitor's display settings, unless you've deliberately scaled it down.
StorageSpaceWarningTooltipFormat: Low on space - Rust's drive only has {0:0}% free. Free up space so Rust's updates have room to download and unpack.
NetworkPageSubtitle: Windows-level network tweaks and a quick connectivity check.
WiredConnectionWarningTooltip: Recommended - a wired (Ethernet) connection has lower and far more consistent latency than Wi-Fi. Use a cable if you can.
OpenSpeedTestLabel: Run a full speed test on Speedtest.net
NetworkThrottlingWarningTooltip: Recommended - Windows caps non-multimedia network traffic by default. Disabling this removes that cap for all traffic.
NicPowerSavingWarningTooltip: Recommended - stops Windows from letting the adapter turn off to save power, which can interrupt connections. A reconnect or reboot may be needed for this to fully apply.
QosReservedBandwidthWarningTooltip: Recommended - Windows can reserve up to 20% of bandwidth for QoS-tagged traffic by default. Disabling this frees it up for everything else.
NetworkPageDisclaimer: These are minor OS-level tweaks, not a fix for a bad connection - they won't help if your ISP, router, or Wi-Fi signal is the real problem. If your ping is spiky under load, look into bufferbloat/QoS on your router first; that matters far more than anything on this page. Your public IP is only looked up if you choose to check it, via a third-party service - nothing else on this page leaves your machine.
ElevationRequiredMessage: Applying network tweaks changes system-wide Windows settings, so Windows will ask you to approve an administrator prompt.
ClearCacheDescription: Clears temporary files, shader caches, Steam download caches and game logs. Rust may take a little longer to start next time while shaders rebuild.
ClearCacheRustSkipped: Rust was running, so shader caches were kept
PresetApplyFailed: Failed to apply preset. Close Rust and try again.
GameplayPageSubtitle: Optional client-side tweaks that improve visibility or clarity, with no performance cost.
RecommendedTweaksSubtitle: Zero downside - safe to leave on regardless of your setup.
PreferenceTweaksSubtitle: These trade something - information, feel, or a little performance - for personal taste. Pick what feels right to you.
TweakSharpenDescription: Enables the sharpening pass, enhancing edge and detail clarity - especially useful since TSSAA softens the image.
TweakHolosightColourDescription: Sets your holosight reticle to a color that stays visible against any background.
TweakTreeMarkerColorDescription: Gives tree resource markers a more visible color on the HUD.
TweakHeadbobDescription: Disables first-person camera bob while walking and running, for a steadier view.
TweakHurtpunchDescription: Disables the screen punch and shake effect when you take damage.
TweakHurtOverlayDescription: Disables the blood/damage overlay shown on screen when you're hit, reducing visual clutter in fights.
TweakLensDirtDescription: Removes the smudge/dirt texture simulated on the camera lens.
TweakVignetteDescription: Removes the darkened screen edges, improving peripheral visibility.
TweakFovDescription: Widens your field of view to 90° for better peripheral awareness.
TweakCrosshairDescription: Hides Rust's built-in crosshair overlay. Tip: try CrosshairY instead.
TweakWorldNotificationsDescription: Shows sound and on-screen toast messages together for world notifications, instead of sound alone.
TweakVmFovScaleDescription: Stops your weapon/hands viewmodel from resizing as your field of view changes.
TweakMaxGibsDescription: Completely removes gib (debris) objects, so combat and explosions don't leave debris behind.
BackupRestorePageSubtitle: Back up and restore Rust's client settings and key bindings.
BackupCreateFailed: Failed to create backup. Make sure Rust is installed.
BackupRestoreFailed: Failed to restore backup. Close Rust and try again.
ConfirmRestoreMessageFormat: This will overwrite your current file with "{0}". A snapshot of what's there now will be taken first, so this can be undone.
ConfirmDeleteMessageFormat: This will permanently delete "{0}". This can't be undone.
ThemeDescription: Follow Windows, or force light or dark
LanguageDescription: The language used throughout the app
StartWithWindowsDescription: Launch Rust Optimizer when Windows starts
SettingsUpdatesSubtitle: Keep Rust Optimizer up to date
CheckForUpdatesOnStartupDescription: Look for a newer version each time the app opens
AutoUpdateDescription: Download and apply updates without asking. The app restarts itself to finish
ThroughputUnitDescription: Bytes match file sizes, bits match how connections are advertised
LogFolderDescription: Open the folder holding the app's log files
LogRetentionDescription: Older log files are deleted automatically
VerboseLoggingDescription: Record everything the app does. Turn this on before reproducing a problem, then send the log
AboutInformationSubtitle: Useful details to include when reporting a problem
AppTagline: Optimize your system and Rust for maximum performance.
